objective: v0.11.0 Tier-2 item 11: decide whether WRF gwd_opt=1 gravity-wave drag is load-bearing for the current Canary d02/nest GPU forecast path.
head: 0cd101cfbf480ae41507ff76e904c6933e899b87

parity.reason: No GPU GWD implementation was added because the active supported GPU path integrates d02/nests from CPU-WRF replay inputs and boundaries, while the CPU-WRF nest wrfinput files carry GWD_OPT=0.

wrf_source_evidence.finding: WRF calls gwdo only when PRESENT(GWD_opt) and GWD_opt == 1.
The ARW first RK step passes config_flags%gwd_opt and VAR/CON/OA/OL fields into the PBL driver.
If a domain namelist config has nonzero gwd_opt, WRF checks it against the domain wrfinput GWD_OPT global attribute.
WRF requires child gwd_opt values to match d01 or be zero; zero-child domains are valid.

gpu_path_evidence.finding: The operational real-case path builds a replay case for a selected domain, defaulting to d02, from existing CPU-WRF artifacts.
OperationalNamelist has damping/diffusion controls but no gwd_opt control.
The runtime applies replay lateral boundaries for the supported real-case d02 path.

reasoning: The CPU-WRF 9 km parent has GWD active and nonzero sub-grid-orography descriptors, so GWD remains load-bearing for any future live d01 parent equivalence claim.
The generated CPU-WRF wrfinput files for d02-d05 carry GWD_OPT=0, so WRF itself does not call the module_bl_gwdo path on the current nests.
The GPU v0.11 operational path is a d02/nest replay path: parent effects are already baked into CPU-WRF initial and boundary artifacts, and no live d01 parent is integrated by the GPU runtime.
Therefore adding a GPU GWD tendency now would not improve WRF faithfulness for current d02/nest forecast quality; it would create an unvalidated extra tendency relative to the CPU-WRF nest baseline.
commands_run: PYTHONPATH=src taskset -c 0-27 python - <<'PY' ... NetCDF4 GWD descriptor stats ... PY
unresolved_risks: This is not a proof that GWD is meteorologically negligible for a future live 9 km d01 parent; it is active there and should be reopened for live multi-domain equivalence.
No GWD parity fixture was built because no implementation was added under this documented-deviation endpoint.
The result relies on the current supported GPU path being d02/nest replay from CPU-WRF artifacts, not a standalone d01-to-d02 nested forecast.
next_decision_needed: For a future live d01 parent release, decide whether to port module_bl_gwdo-style GWD or require a manager-approved explicit namelist deviation for d01.
